completed_tasks.name: Feature Velocity page — full pipeline with real BQ data

remaining_tasks.name: SDK Versions page — cli_version/local_version donut + table
Engagement Decay page — cold-detection table + sparklines
User Journey page — Sankey from dim_users first_*_at fields

human_actions_pending.context: team_or_profile_viewed events dropped from 32 users to 2 overnight — customer-specific, not platform-wide

decisions.decision: Separate pages per direction (not tabs in existing reports)
decisions.rationale: Enables parallel prototyping, each independently deployable

decisions.decision: All 9 directions + all differentiators in v1 scope
decisions.rationale: User wants full build, 82 requirements total

decisions.decision: V4 sparkline design: gold bars + event count labels above + user count below in green
decisions.rationale: Iterated through 4 options — lines were misleading, dots were messy, numbers are cleanest
Ghost users appeared with null events but non-null user counts

decisions.decision: Weave suppression via account_health_query weave_customer flag
decisions.rationale: Non-Weave customers showed Weave data from trials
Isomorphic Labs collaboration cliff showed need to distinguish instrumentation changes from behavior

decisions.decision: Always use real data for prototyping, never fake data
decisions.rationale: User feedback — fake data is useless for validating visualizations

decisions.decision: Show deployment type and data provenance on pages
decisions.rationale: Server deployment customers have small SaaS footprints that aren't representative

next_action: Build SDK Versions page (easiest remaining Phase 2 page) following the same pattern: add query, create transform, add renderer to base template, generate with real data for Isomorphic Labs, iterate visually
context_notes: Phase 1 foundation is complete but GSD phase tracking is slightly out of sync — we pulled Feature Velocity forward from Phase 2 during Phase 1 visual verification. The PAGE_RENDERERS pattern in base-template.html dispatches by page_type, so adding new pages means: 1) handler in generate.py, 2) transform class, 3) renderer function in template JS, 4) entry in PAGE_RENDERERS. V1-V3 renderer code for feature velocity is still in the template (dead code) — clean up when stabilized.
